title: 2026 Nevada gubernatorial election
description: Nevada will elect its next governor in 2026, with the primary scheduled for June 9 and the general election on November 3. The race is expected to be competitive in a closely divided swing state, and the governor will shape policy on housing affordability, taxes, education, public safety, and reproductive rights. Incumbent Republican Joe Lombardo is seeking reelection, while Democratic Attorney General Aaron Ford is a leading challenger; Washoe County Commission Chair Alexis Hill is also running. Early polling has shown a close contest between Lombardo and Ford, including an Emerson College Polling survey conducted November 16-18, 2025 that found them tied at 41% each.

candidates.name: Joe Lombardo
candidates.summary: Joe Lombardo is the Republican governor of Nevada and a former Clark County sheriff. He was elected governor in 2022 and is seeking reelection in 2026, with his public profile centered on public safety, education, housing, and economic development.

career_history.title: Sheriff
career_history.description: Elected sheriff of Clark County in 2014 and re-elected in 2018.

career_history.title: Assistant sheriff and other ranks
career_history.description: Served for 26 years in LVMPD, rising through multiple ranks.

career_history.title: Soldier
career_history.organization: United States Army and Nevada National Guard
career_history.description: Served in the U.S. Army and Nevada National Guard after college.

voting_record.bill_description: Joe Lombardo is governor and does not have a legislative roll-call voting record.

top_donors.name: Southern Nevada Building & Construction Trades Council
NV Republican Party Central Committee Non-Federal Victory

Healthcare.stance: Lombardo says he supports expanding access to affordable care and created the Nevada Health Authority to streamline state health programs. His campaign also says he supports contraceptive access and remains pro-life, while leaving abortion policy to Nevada voters.

sources.title: Governor Lombardo Announces Official Launch of the Nevada Health Authority
Reproductive Health — Joe Lombardo for Governor

Economy.stance: Lombardo says his administration has focused on tax relief, reduced regulation, workforce development, and attracting private investment to diversify Nevada’s economy. His campaign highlights lower business taxes, fewer regulations, and major new investment and job growth as core results.

sources.title: Governor Lombardo Highlights Record of Results, Outlines Continued Priorities at Vegas Chamber Luncheon

Climate/Energy.stance: Lombardo supports an all-of-the-above energy approach that includes renewables, natural gas, and other reliable sources. He has also promoted Nevada’s Climate Innovation Plan and said he wants to expand energy production while supporting economic growth.

sources.title: Governor Lombardo Announces Nevada Climate Innovation Plan

Reproductive Rights.stance: Lombardo says he is Catholic and pro-life, but that abortion policy should be decided by Nevada voters rather than imposed by politicians. His campaign also says he supports access to contraceptives as an essential part of healthcare and wants contraceptives to remain accessible in Nevada.

sources.title: Reproductive Health — Joe Lombardo for Governor
Nevada Republican governor approves abortion protections in cross-party move - AP News

Immigration.stance: Lombardo says Nevada should follow federal immigration law and not be a sanctuary state. His administration has also said it will cooperate with federal authorities on immigration enforcement.

sources.title: DOJ and State of Nevada Sign Immigration Enforcement Agreement
Justice Department Announces Memorandum of Understanding with Nevada to Collaborate on Immigration

Guns & Safety.stance: Lombardo says he supports the Second Amendment, opposes gun registries, and has vetoed several gun-control bills. His campaign and news coverage also show support for targeted public-safety measures and tougher penalties for violent crime.

sources.title: Second Amendment — Joe Lombardo for Governor
Does Joe Lombardo want to repeal Nevada's red flag law?
Why Did Nevada's Governor Veto a Ban on Ghost Guns?
Governor Lombardo Ceremonially Signs Nevada Safe Streets and Neighborhoods Act

Foreign Policy.stance: Lombardo has publicly described Iran as a threat to the United States and Israel and said Iran must never obtain a nuclear weapon. After U.S. strikes on Iran, he called for a swift resolution and said Nevada law enforcement would coordinate with federal partners to protect public safety.

sources.title: Governor Joe Lombardo addresses U.S. attack on Iran
Lombardo says ‘certain locations’ could see increased police presence amid strikes on Iran

Social Justice.stance: Lombardo is backing a 2026 ballot initiative to bar transgender girls and women from girls’ and women’s sports in Nevada. He says the proposal is about fairness and safety, while critics argue it would discriminate against transgender students.

sources.title: Lombardo leads 2026 ballot initiative to block students born male from female sports
Judge rules Lombardo's trans athlete ballot initiative can proceed

Education.stance: Lombardo says he has prioritized school choice, teacher pay, accountability, and literacy initiatives. His campaign says Nevada has expanded charter seats, funded education more heavily, and opted into a federal tax credit scholarship program under his administration.

sources.title: Governor Lombardo Opts-In To Federal Tax Credit Scholarship Program

Tech & AI.stance: Lombardo signed a 2025 law expanding Nevada’s child pornography statutes to cover AI-generated or AI-modified images. That action shows support for regulating harmful AI content, but his campaign does not appear to have a broader public AI platform.

sources.title: Nevada governor signs law banning child porn and revenge porn made by AI
Governor Lombardo signs bill combatting child pornography generated by artificial intelligence

Election Reform.stance: Lombardo says election administration should remain a state issue and has supported voter ID in principle, but he vetoed a 2025 voter-ID bill because of implementation concerns. He has also opposed efforts he views as nationalizing elections.

sources.title: Nevada voter ID bill is vetoed by Republican governor
IndyTalks: Lombardo pushes against nationalizing elections, calls it 'state issue'

Local Issues.stance: Lombardo has centered his local agenda on public safety, housing affordability, and state services. His administration highlights the Nevada Safe Streets and Neighborhoods Act, housing initiatives, and efforts to reduce homelessness and improve government efficiency.

sources.title: Governor Lombardo Ceremonially Signs Nevada Safe Streets and Neighborhoods Act

candidates.donor_summary: Joe Lombardo’s fundraising is dominated by business and industry donors, including real estate, construction, and auto-related interests. OpenSecrets is the best source for detailed donor breakdowns, and campaign reporting indicates he entered 2026 with substantial cash on hand.
candidates.voting_summary: Joe Lombardo is an incumbent governor, not a legislator, so he does not have a legislative roll-call voting record. His comparable record is his executive tenure as Nevada governor, where he has used vetoes and signed legislation on education, public safety, housing, and health care.

candidates.name: Aaron Ford
candidates.summary: Aaron Ford is Nevada’s attorney general and a Democratic candidate for governor in 2026. He has said he earned five degrees from The Ohio State University, including a law degree and a Ph.D. in Educational Administration, and previously served in the Nevada State Senate and as a public school math teacher.

summary_sources.title: AG Aaron Ford - Nevada Attorney General
About — Aaron Ford for Nevada Governor

career_history.description: Served as a state senator and held majority leader and minority leader roles.

career_history.title: Partner
career_history.description: Worked in private practice as a partner.

career_history.title: Partner
career_history.description: Worked in private practice as a partner.

career_history.title: Public school math teacher
career_history.description: Taught math before entering law and politics.

Aaron D Ford Top Contributors - OpenSecrets

Aaron D Ford Top Contributors - OpenSecrets
Aaron D Ford Top Contributors - OpenSecrets

Healthcare.stance: Ford says he would crack down on pharmacy benefit managers so prescription discounts and rebates reach consumers, relieve medical debt through a state debt-relief program, and target price gouging and fraudulent medical billing to lower costs.

Economy.stance: Ford’s economic message centers on affordability, especially housing. He supports cracking down on corporate homebuying and Wall Street investors, easing permitting, using vacant land for development, and opposing tariffs he says raise costs for Nevada families.

sources.title: Ford intros housing plan with rental aid programs, crackdown on corporate homebuying

Climate/Energy.stance: Ford says he would fight utility cost increases, support clean and affordable energy, expand residential solar and energy-efficiency incentives, and require data centers to pay their fair share for increased energy demand while conserving water.

Reproductive Rights.stance: Ford’s campaign and attorney general record emphasize protecting reproductive freedom and access to contraception. His office has also joined litigation and coalition efforts defending access to birth control and other contraceptive care.

sources.title: About — Aaron Ford for Nevada Governor
Attorney General Ford Defends Access to Birth Control and Other Contraceptive Care

Immigration.stance: Ford has said his office’s model immigration policies are not intended to create a sanctuary state and are meant to guide law enforcement, courts, and higher education while emphasizing public safety.

sources.title: Attorney General Aaron Ford Releases Response to Governor Lombardo’s Misleading Statement on Model Immigration Policies
Attorney General Ford Announces Publication of Model Immigration Policies

Guns & Safety.stance: Ford’s public record emphasizes gun-violence prevention and public safety, including litigation to block distribution of machine gun conversion devices and campaign language about cracking down on ghost guns and other dangerous weapons.

sources.title: Attorney General Ford Sues Trump Administration to Prevent Efforts to Distribute Thousands of Machine Gun Devices Across Country
About — Aaron Ford for Nevada Governor

Foreign Policy.stance: No public position found on a Nevada-specific foreign policy platform. His public statements in the materials reviewed focus on state issues and on opposing federal actions such as tariffs.

sources.title: Attorney General Ford Seeks Court Order to Stop the Trump Administration’s Illegal Tariffs

Social Justice.stance: Ford has highlighted hate-crimes enforcement, anti-discrimination efforts, and fair-housing litigation. His office announced Nevada’s Hate Crimes Task Force and joined coalitions opposing rollbacks of fair-housing regulations.

sources.title: Attorney General Ford Announces Inaugural Meeting of Nevada Hate Crimes Task Force
Attorney General Ford Joins Coalition Opposing Rollback of Crucial Fair Housing Regulations

Education.stance: Ford says he would support stronger funding for public schools, continue education roundtables with parents and educators, and defend federal education funding. His campaign has also said he supports guaranteeing meals for public school students.

sources.title: Aaron Ford Holds Education Roundtable with Southern Nevada Educators, Parents, and Students
Attorney General Ford Secures Agreement with Trump Administration to Protect Millions in Education Funding Related to DEIA Initiatives

Tech & AI.stance: Ford’s public record emphasizes cybercrime enforcement and consumer protection online. His office joined the FBI-led Las Vegas Cyber Task Force, but no detailed AI-specific policy platform was found in the reviewed sources.

sources.title: Attorney General Ford Announces Nevada Has Joined Cyber Task Force

Election Reform.stance: No public position found on election reform in the sources reviewed. The campaign materials examined focused instead on affordability, housing, energy, health care, and public safety.

Local Issues.stance: Ford’s main local issue is housing affordability. He supports capping security deposits at one month’s rent, banning junk fees, expanding rental and down payment assistance, easing permitting, and using vacant land and federal land for affordable housing.

sources.title: Ford intros housing plan with rental aid programs, crackdown on corporate homebuying

candidates.donor_summary: Aaron Ford’s fundraising has come from a mix of labor organizations, lawyers, and other professional donors, according to OpenSecrets. The profile’s donor data points to a broad donor base rather than a single dominant industry.
candidates.voting_summary: Aaron Ford is Nevada’s attorney general, not a legislator, so he does not have a legislative roll-call voting record. His public record is instead reflected in executive and legal actions taken as attorney general.

candidates.name: Matthew Winterhawk
candidates.summary: Matthew Winterhawk is a Republican candidate for governor of Nevada in 2026. His campaign describes him as a father of two, husband, business operator, nonprofit founder, and civic reformer, and Ballotpedia identifies him as a candidate in the Republican primary on June 9, 2026.

summary_sources.title: About Matthew Winterhawk | Nevada Governor Candidate 2026

voting_record.bill_description: Matthew Winterhawk is a gubernatorial candidate with no identified legislative roll-call voting record.

Healthcare.stance: Winterhawk does not appear to have a broad public healthcare platform. His published materials emphasize parental consent and parental direction over children’s upbringing, education, healthcare, and values, and say medical, social, or psychological intervention in children should not occur without parental consent.

sources.title: NPPA - Matthew Winterhawk for Governor of Nevada (2026)

Economy.stance: Winterhawk says he would create an Office of Government Waste & Accountability and a Department of Government Efficiency to audit agencies, publish spending dashboards, and reduce waste. He also says he would freeze non-emergency regulations, review costs, and replace underperforming agency leadership to improve conditions for small businesses and local ownership.

sources.title: First 100 Days as Governor | Winterhawk Executive Action Plan
How the Nevada Executive Branch Works | Winterhawk Transparency Plan

Climate/Energy.stance: Winterhawk frames climate and energy policy through Nevada sovereignty over land and water. He supports auditing federal water contracts and advancing the Nevada Land Sovereignty and Reclamation Act to keep land- and resource-related revenues in Nevada and fund drought mitigation and conservation.

sources.title: Nevada Land Sovereignty and Reclamation Act (NLSARA)

Reproductive Rights.stance: No public position found on abortion or broader reproductive-rights policy. His published materials instead focus on parental consent and restrictions on medical or social interventions involving minors.

sources.title: NPPA - Matthew Winterhawk for Governor of Nevada (2026)

Immigration.stance: Winterhawk says Nevada’s laws, schools, hospitals, and public services should prioritize legal residents, citizens, and people here lawfully. He opposes sanctuary policies, supports secure ID for voting and benefits, and calls for coordination with sheriffs and federal agencies to remove criminal offenders through proper channels.

sources.title: Nevada Land Sovereignty and Reclamation Act (NLSARA)

Guns & Safety.stance: Winterhawk does not appear to have a standalone firearms policy. His public safety agenda emphasizes zero tolerance for violent and repeat offenders, stronger penalties for organized retail crime and trafficking, school safety, and victim support.

Foreign Policy.stance: No public position found on foreign policy. His public agenda focuses on Nevada sovereignty, state control over land and resources, and state governance issues rather than international affairs.

sources.title: Nevada Land Sovereignty and Reclamation Act (NLSARA)

Social Justice.stance: Winterhawk’s platform emphasizes parental rights, education accountability, public safety, election integrity, and government transparency rather than a broad social-justice agenda. His materials also prioritize legal residents in public services and support stronger voting safeguards.

Education.stance: Winterhawk says Nevada classrooms should teach skills, literacy, critical thinking, civics, and constitutional education rather than political or corporate agendas. He supports parental rights, curriculum transparency, age-appropriate materials, and removing adult political content from schools.

sources.title: About Matthew Winterhawk | Nevada Governor Candidate 2026

Tech & AI.stance: Winterhawk’s NIDDA proposal would require apps and websites to disclose data collection, ban sale or transfer of biometric data without explicit consent, create a Nevada data privacy agency, and label ads that use behavioral tracking or AI-generated targeting.

Election Reform.stance: Winterhawk supports mandatory voter ID, prohibiting non-citizen voting, ending universal mail ballots, requiring paper trails and independent audits, cleaning voter rolls, and county-level tabulation oversight.

Local Issues.stance: Winterhawk says Nevada land and water should remain under Nevada control. He supports auditing federal water contracts, prioritizing agriculture and conservation, and increasing transparency in development approvals through the Nevada Land Sovereignty and Reclamation Act.

sources.title: Nevada Land Sovereignty and Reclamation Act (NLSARA)

candidates.donor_summary: Winterhawk says his campaign operates without PAC money, corporate donors, or financial gatekeepers, and his campaign materials emphasize direct public support over traditional fundraising. Public finance portals are available, but the campaign site does not provide a detailed donor breakdown.
candidates.voting_summary: Matthew Winterhawk has no public legislative voting record because he is not a current or former legislator. Available evidence of his governing approach comes from his campaign platform, which emphasizes transparency, accountability, public safety, and education reform.

candidates.name: Irina Hansen
candidates.summary: Irina Hansen is a Republican candidate for Nevada governor in 2026. Her campaign says she is a longtime real estate agent, salon and spa owner, and mother of three, and that she is running to restore government accountability, strengthen the economy, improve public safety, and expand school choice. Her campaign website also says it has not published a detailed public platform beyond its issue pages and priorities page.

candidates.donor_summary: No public campaign finance data or separate donor disclosure page was found for Irina Hansen in the reviewed sources. The campaign website and related candidate pages were available, but no verified OpenSecrets, FEC, or Nevada state disclosure totals for her gubernatorial campaign were identified.
candidates.voting_summary: Irina Hansen has no public legislative voting record because she is not a current or former legislator. The available public record instead consists of her campaign platform and prior local-campaign materials.

About Irina Hansen

Economy.stance: Irina Hansen says she wants to strengthen the economy by supporting small businesses, reducing unnecessary regulation, and expanding access to capital through grants and microloans. Her campaign also proposes a Nevada Small Business Hub and fee reductions for some new entrepreneurs.

sources.title: Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

Immigration.stance: Irina Hansen says she wants to end sanctuary policies, enforce the rule of law, and support legal immigrants by prioritizing their access to resources and services. Her campaign frames this as stricter enforcement paired with support for legal immigration.

sources.title: END SANCTUARY POLICIES - Irina Hansen for Nevada Governor
Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

Guns & Safety.stance: Irina Hansen says she will defend the Second Amendment without compromise and oppose restrictions on law-abiding Nevadans' right to keep and bear arms. Her campaign also says she would prioritize law enforcement funding and stronger penalties for violent and repeat offenders.

sources.title: SECOND AMENDMENT - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor
Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

Election Reform.stance: Irina Hansen advocates voter ID and election-integrity measures, including government-issued photo ID to vote, regular voter-roll audits, and a ban on ballot harvesting. Her campaign also calls for stricter election security and restoring public trust in Nevada elections.

sources.title: ELECTION INTEGRITY - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor
Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

Local Issues.stance: Irina Hansen's local-issues platform centers on affordable housing, anti-corruption enforcement, and government accountability. She says she would audit housing-fund fraud, streamline development, tie funding to measurable results, and address homelessness through treatment, mental health support, and job training.

sources.title: Affordable Housing - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor
Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

Education.stance: Irina Hansen says she supports increasing funding for school choice, allowing education dollars to follow the student to public, charter, private, or homeschool options, expanding vocational and career training, and giving parents more say in their children's education.

sources.title: Education - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

candidates.name: Alexis Hill
candidates.summary: Alexis Hill is the chair of the Washoe County Commission and a Democratic candidate for Nevada governor in 2026. Her background includes more than a decade in municipal public service, and her campaign emphasizes tax reform, housing affordability, schools, infrastructure, and public services.

summary_sources.title: About Alexis - Alexis Hill for Nevada Governor

career_history.title: Chair, Washoe County Commission
career_history.description: Represents District 1 and serves as chair of the five-member county commission.

career_history.title: Public policy and city planning professional
career_history.description: Worked in municipal public policy and city planning for more than a decade before joining county government.

candidates.donor_summary: Alexis Hill’s campaign finance reporting is available through the Nevada Secretary of State portal. Public filings are the best source for exact totals and contributors, and recent coverage notes she has been running with a fundraising disadvantage against Joe Lombardo.
candidates.voting_summary: Alexis Hill is a Washoe County commissioner, not a state legislator, so she does not have a legislative voting record. Her comparable governing record is at the county level, where official sources identify her as the Washoe County Commission chair.

Washoe County Commissioner Alexis Hill explains why ...
Alexis Hill | Nevada Governor 2026
Lombardo has sevenfold cash advantage over closest opponent
About Alexis - Alexis Hill for Nevada Governor

Economy.stance: Hill says Nevada should modernize its tax system, end tax giveaways to large corporations and billionaires, and invest in workforce development and local businesses instead of relying on subsidies for major companies. She also proposes property-tax and road-funding reforms to raise sustainable revenue for public services.

sources.title: Democrat Alexis Hill jumps into Nevada governor race, unveils revenue-raising tax plan

Education.stance: Hill says Nevada should close its education funding gap and reach the national average, which she says would require about $2.5 billion in additional annual spending over the next decade. She supports universal pre-K, free school breakfast and lunch, higher teacher pay, and expanded workforce and career training.

Healthcare.stance: Hill supports expanding access to care by increasing Medicaid reimbursement rates, creating a mental health division within the Nevada Department of Human Services, and funding the state public option. Her campaign also says Nevada should make state health plans available more broadly and invest in long-term mental-health-focused care.

Immigration.stance: Hill says immigration enforcement should remain a federal responsibility, opposes costly state-run detention centers, and does not support using the Nevada National Guard to indiscriminately round up immigrants. She says Nevada should partner with federal agencies, protect due process, and support comprehensive reform with pathways to citizenship.

sources.title: Is Nevada sanctuary state? See what new law says on access to schools

Foreign Policy.stance: No public position found on foreign policy in the campaign materials reviewed.

Election Reform.stance: Hill says Nevada should strengthen voter protections, protect voter information, speed up election-result reporting, and ensure registered nonpartisans have fair access to primary elections.

Local Issues.stance: Hill’s campaign focuses on housing affordability, roads, homelessness, childcare, public safety, parks, and infrastructure. She says state and local governments should fund these services sustainably rather than relying on temporary fixes or corporate subsidies.

Reproductive Rights.stance: Hill says she is a staunch supporter of the right to choose and has said she was endorsed by Planned Parenthood Action in prior runs for office. Her campaign frames abortion access as part of a broader privacy and personal-freedom platform.

Climate/Energy.stance: Hill says protecting Nevada’s air, water, land, and climate is fundamental to quality of life, and she points to local sustainability planning as part of her record. Her campaign links environmental protection to long-term planning, conservation, and public investment.

Tech & AI.stance: Hill says Nevada should modernize its digital infrastructure and establish a statewide AI governance framework for state agencies. Her campaign also proposes pausing new corporate tax abatements for data centers and regulating their energy use.

Guns & Safety.stance: Hill says public safety should be sustainably funded for police, firefighters, and other first responders. Her campaign also ties safety to infrastructure, homelessness response, and community investment rather than focusing only on criminal-justice enforcement.

Social Justice.stance: Hill says she supports LGBTQ+ rights, Native American sovereignty, privacy rights, workers’ rights, and fair wages. She also supports policies to make housing, childcare, healthcare, and voting more accessible and affordable.

sources.title: Democrat Alexis Hill jumps into Nevada governor race, unveils revenue-raising tax plan

candidates.name: Donald J. Beaudry Jr.
candidates.summary: Donald J. Beaudry Jr. is a Republican candidate for governor of Nevada in 2026 who says he is based in Las Vegas. His campaign describes him as the founder and CEO of BlkEdgeAi and says he is running on a plan to replace Nevada state taxes with revenue generated through a state-owned financial exchange called NGEX.

summary_sources.title: Donald Jr. Governor of Nevada Candidate 2026

candidates.donor_summary: No campaign finance data found in public disclosures as of 2026-04-07. The reviewed sources included his campaign website and Ballotpedia page, but no OpenSecrets, FEC, or state disclosure totals were surfaced in the search results.
candidates.voting_summary: Donald J. Beaudry Jr. has no public legislative voting record found in the sources reviewed. He appears to be a non-legislator candidate; the available evidence is his campaign’s fiscal-reform platform and self-description as a financial markets engineer and founder.

candidates.name: Danielle Ford
candidates.summary: Danielle Ford is a Las Vegas-based marketing strategist and elected member of Nevada’s State Board of Education. She filed to run for governor of Nevada in 2026 as a nonpartisan candidate, and her public campaign materials do not appear to include a detailed policy platform beyond her education-focused background.

summary_sources.title: Ford files for governor (no, not THAT Ford) - KTNV

career_history.title: Member
career_history.description: Elected member of the Nevada State Board of Education; her campaign bio says she was elected to the board in 2023 and advocates for real change and transparency in public education.

candidates.donor_summary: Danielle Ford’s campaign finance reporting is available through the Nevada Secretary of State candidate disclosure portal, but the reviewed search snippets did not surface a precise total raised. Transparency USA and the state filing page are the best sources for her donor breakdown and filing history.
candidates.voting_summary: Danielle Ford is a member of Nevada’s State Board of Education, not a legislator, so there is no legislative voting record. Her comparable public record is board-level governance; the reviewed sources confirm she is a first-term board member and gubernatorial candidate, but they do not provide a detailed vote-by-vote record.

candidates.name: M. Kameron Hawkins
candidates.summary: M. Kameron Hawkins is a Republican candidate for governor of Nevada in 2026. His campaign website describes him as a Nevada business and nonprofit leader and a U.S. Navy veteran, and says he announced his gubernatorial bid on February 23, 2023.

summary_sources.title: Kameron Hawkins – Candidate Hawkins for Governor of Nevada 2026

career_history.title: Founder/Chairman & Volunteer
career_history.description: Founded and leads the nonprofit organization described on his campaign site as supporting other nonprofits and businesses; campaign materials also identify him as a U.S. Navy veteran.

career_history.title: United States Navy veteran
career_history.description: Served in the U.S. Navy, with his first duty station in Washington, D.C., from 1983 to 1986; campaign biography says he was part of the Presidential Ceremonial Guard Drill Team Unit.

Economy.stance: Hawkins says Nevada should diversify beyond tourism and gaming, with targeted incentives for renewable energy, advanced manufacturing, and biotechnology, plus zoning reforms and public-private partnerships to expand affordable housing.

Education.stance: Hawkins says Nevada needs stronger education accountability, more stable K-12 funding, competitive teacher pay, expanded early childhood education, and modernized vocational-technical programs.

Healthcare.stance: Hawkins says Nevada should address its shortage of medical professionals by expanding medical education and residency slots, using loan forgiveness and practice grants, and strengthening telehealth and community health centers, especially in rural areas.

Local Issues.stance: Hawkins frames his campaign around listening to local communities and addressing Nevada-specific concerns including economic development, education, healthcare, water, and tourism.

sources.title: Identified Nevada Challenges and Potential Solutions

Climate/Energy.stance: Hawkins says Nevada must balance growth with water scarcity and protect natural resources, but the reviewed campaign materials do not lay out a detailed climate or energy policy.

candidates.name: Jose M. Zelaya
candidates.summary: Jose M. Zelaya is a Republican candidate for governor of Nevada in 2026. His campaign website says he is running on lowering the cost of living, expanding housing supply, supporting small businesses, improving education, making healthcare more affordable, securing Nevada’s water future, and investing in clean energy and new industries.

summary_sources.title: 2026 Candidate Public Media Information | Nevada Secretary of State

Jose Zelaya For Nevada Instagram
Jose Zelaya YouTube

Healthcare.stance: Zelaya says he would make healthcare more affordable by reducing prescription drug costs, expanding access in rural communities, strengthening the mental health workforce, improving Medicaid efficiency, and promoting preventive care.

Economy.stance: Zelaya says he would lower the cost of living, deliver targeted tax relief, support small businesses, expand workforce training and apprenticeships, and cut red tape for entrepreneurs.

Climate/Energy.stance: Zelaya says he would support clean energy and new industries, including expanding Nevada’s lithium and battery production economy, supporting solar and energy storage projects, modernizing the grid, and attracting advanced manufacturing and technology investment.

Guns & Safety.stance: Zelaya says he would provide strong support, training, and resources for law enforcement, take action against retail theft, drug trafficking, and violent crime, expand community policing partnerships, and improve mental health crisis response systems.

Education.stance: Zelaya says he would increase teacher pay, send more funding directly to classrooms, expand career and technical education, strengthen early literacy programs, and improve school accountability.

candidates.name: Barak Zilberberg
candidates.summary: Barak Zilberberg is a Las Vegas-based real estate investor and entrepreneur running as a Republican for governor of Nevada in 2026. His campaign website describes him as a self-made entrepreneur, investor, philanthropist, husband, and father, and says he previously ran for governor in 2022.

career_history.title: Real estate investor and entrepreneur
career_history.description: Campaign website describes him as a real estate investor and entrepreneur who has worked in real estate ventures for more than 30 years.

candidates.donor_summary: No campaign finance data found in public disclosures as of 2026-04-07. The reviewed search results surfaced Ballotpedia and social/campaign references, but no OpenSecrets, FEC, or state disclosure totals were found in the search snippets.
candidates.voting_summary: No public legislative voting record. No prior elected or executive office found in the sources reviewed, so there is no comparable voting or governing record to summarize.

Healthcare.stance: He lists healthcare as one of his campaign's pros and says it is a priority, but the campaign site does not provide a detailed healthcare policy proposal.

Economy.stance: He emphasizes entrepreneurship, small business, and running government with more accountability and self-sufficiency. On his issue page, he links inflation and budget concerns to producing more goods and services domestically and reducing debt.

Education.stance: He says education is a priority, lists school choice among his campaign’s pros, and says he would donate his salary to children’s education.

Social Justice.stance: He says all people are created equal regardless of race, religion, color, gender, or sexual orientation, and that he supports transgender and LGBTQ communities.

Local Issues.stance: His campaign is framed around Nevada-specific concerns, especially healthcare, education, jobs, and affordability, and his website identifies him as a Las Vegas-based candidate focused on the state.

Climate/Energy.stance: He says people should not harm the environment and includes the environment in his transgender/equality statement, but the campaign site does not lay out a specific climate or energy policy.

Reproductive Rights.stance: No specific public position found on reproductive rights in the campaign materials reviewed.

Immigration.stance: He says the U.S. should secure its borders and allow legal immigration through a vetted process, including a work permit, Social Security number, and a path to permanent residency and citizenship over time.

Guns & Safety.stance: He lists support for the Second Amendment among his campaign’s pros, but the campaign site does not provide a detailed firearms or public-safety policy.

Foreign Policy.stance: He says he favors peace over war, wants negotiations to end conflicts, supports strong relations with NATO allies, and argues the U.S. should invest in military and cybersecurity while avoiding nuclear conflict.

Tech & AI.stance: He does not appear to have a specific public AI policy, but he says the U.S. should invest heavily in cybersecurity and protect data systems and servers from intrusion.

Election Reform.stance: No specific public position found on election reform in the campaign materials reviewed.

flags.concern: The Emerson College Polling entry dated 2025-11-18 is duplicated five times (entries 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 all show identical data: Ford 41%, Lombardo 41%, sample size 800). A single consolidated entry should replace these duplicates.
flags.suggestion: Remove the four duplicate Emerson entries and retain only one entry with the same data.

flags.concern: Southern Nevada Building & Construction Trades Council is listed with 'amount: null' despite being a top donor. The source URL provided (OpenSecrets) should clarify whether the donation amount is unknown or if this entry should be removed from the top-donor list.
flags.suggestion: Either populate the amount field from the source URL or remove this entry if the amount cannot be verified.

flags.concern: NV Republican Party Central Committee Non-Federal Victory is listed with 'amount: null'. As with the prior donor entry, the source should clarify whether an amount exists.
flags.suggestion: Either populate the amount field or remove the entry.

flags.concern: Henderson Nissan is listed with 'amount: null'. Consistency with donor-entry standards suggests clarification or removal.
flags.suggestion: Either populate the amount field or remove the entry.

flags.concern: Matthew Winterhawk's career_history array is empty, despite his campaign bio describing him as a 'business operator' and 'civic reformer.' The profile should document any prior roles or professional experience cited in his campaign materials.
flags.suggestion: Review his campaign site (https://w4nevada.com/biography) and add documented career history entries if available.

flags.concern: Matthew Winterhawk's education array is empty. His campaign materials or public filings may contain education information that should be included if available.
flags.suggestion: Check campaign biography or public records for education details and add if found.

flags.concern: Irina Hansen's education array is empty. The profile summary describes her as a 'real estate agent' and 'salon and spa owner' but does not document formal education.
flags.suggestion: Verify whether education information is publicly available and add if found.

flags.concern: Irina Hansen's career_history array is empty, though the summary mentions she is a 'real estate agent, salon and spa owner.' These roles should be documented with start/end years if available.
flags.suggestion: Add documented career entries based on campaign or public sources.

flags.concern: Sunshine Arterburn has a voting_record entry claiming she is a 'gubernatorial candidate with no identified legislative roll-call voting record,' but the entry has no bill_name, description, vote, or date fields populated. This entry is malformed and should be removed or completed.
flags.suggestion: Remove the empty/malformed voting_record entry, or populate it with actual data if available.

flags.concern: Christopher Battenberg has a similar malformed voting_record entry with no substantive data. Remove or complete.
flags.suggestion: Remove the empty voting_record entry.

flags.concern: All issues for Miqehl Bayfield show 'low' or 'high' confidence with proper sourcing, which is good. However, 'Immigration' is marked as 'No public position found' without any attempt to source it, while other candidates have sources cited for null findings. For consistency, if no immigration position exists, cite the source page(s) checked.
flags.suggestion: Add source URLs to confirm immigration policy was searched but not found.

flags.concern: Donald J. Beaudry Jr.'s entire issues section relies heavily on his campaign website (https://www.djr26gov.com/) for all sourcing. No independent news verification or third-party fact-checking is cited. While the website is a valid source, corroboration would strengthen credibility.
flags.suggestion: Consider adding news coverage or other independent sources to verify or contextualize his fiscal-reform proposals.

flags.concern: Danielle Ford's Reproductive Rights stance shows 'No public position found,' but the reviewed sources URL paths (e.g., https://danielleford.com/about/issues with a 404 note) suggest that pages were checked but may no longer be live. The 404 result should be documented as a source verification issue rather than omitted.
flags.suggestion: Note in the sources that the issues page returned a 404 or was inaccessible, rather than implying the page was never searched.

flags.concern: M. Kameron Hawkins has no image_url value, while most other candidates have Ballotpedia or Wikipedia images. This is a minor omission but reduces profile completeness.
flags.suggestion: Check Ballotpedia or news sources for a public candidate image if available.

flags.concern: Allen Rheinhart's image_url is listed as 'https://s3.amazonaws.com/ballotpedia-api4/files/thumbs/200/300/arheinhart3.jpg', but the campaign website (https://www.allenrheinhart.com/issues) was marked as having a placeholder issues page. The image URL should be spot-checked to ensure it is not a placeholder or placeholder-like image.
flags.suggestion: Verify that the image URL points to an actual candidate photo.

flags.concern: Emilio R. Rodriguez's website field is set to 'https://wenevada.net/', but the profile notes that no public policy positions or detailed biographical information were found in the sources reviewed. The website URL may exist but lack substantive content, or it may be under construction.
flags.suggestion: Confirm the website is live and note its current status (e.g., 'minimal platform,' 'under construction') in the summary or sourcing.

flags.concern: Aaron Ford's profile states he earned 'five degrees from The Ohio State University,' but only four degrees are listed in the education array: Ph.D. (Educational Administration), J.D., Bachelor's (Interdisciplinary studies), and Master's (International education). The count does not match the summary claim of five degrees.
flags.suggestion: Verify the exact number of degrees Ford earned. Either update the summary to 'four degrees' or add the missing fifth degree entry if it exists.

flags.concern: The description mentions 'Washoe County Commission Chair Alexis Hill is also running' but does not identify her party affiliation in this summary sentence. While she is listed as Democratic in her candidate profile, the opening description should clarify whether she is a Democratic challenger, for consistency with the Aaron Ford mention.
flags.suggestion: Update description to specify Hill's Democratic affiliation, or restructure to make it clear the race includes challengers from both major parties.

reviews.summary: The profile demonstrates solid sourcing on major candidates (Lombardo, Ford, Hill) with well-documented career histories and issue positions tied to official campaign sites and credible news outlets. However, several structural issues and gaps reduce overall quality: (1) the polling section contains five duplicate Emerson entries that should be consolidated; (2) several minor candidates have extremely sparse biographical and platform information, which is acceptable given their obscurity, but some carry placeholder-like or unverified education/career data; (3) a small number of unsourced claims lack explicit hedging about verification status; and (4) language is generally neutral, though some issue framings slightly favor candidate rhetoric without independent context. The profile is usable but would benefit from deduplication, clearer sourcing on fringe candidates, and minor fact-checking on education claims.

flags.concern: The polling array contains five identical entries for the Emerson College survey dated 2025-11-18.
flags.suggestion: Consolidate the duplicate Emerson College Polling entries into a single record to improve readability.

flags.concern: The links for candidate Irina Hansen (candidates[3].links[4] and [5]) point to OpenSecrets profiles for 'Ira Hansen.' My training data suggests Ira Hansen is a Nevada State Senator and a different individual than Irina Hansen, though they are related. Using his financial data for her profile may be misleading for a human reviewer.

reviews.summary: The profile provides an impressively deep dive into a very crowded 20-candidate field for the 2026 Nevada Gubernatorial race. It excels at locating primary campaign materials for minor and niche candidates that are often overlooked, such as Matthew Winterhawk's NIDDA proposal and Donald Beaudry's NGEX fiscal plan. Sourcing is robust, using a mix of official state records (NV SOS), local investigative news (The Nevada Independent, Nevada Current), and primary campaign sites. Stances are reported neutrally with appropriate confidence levels that reflect the public availability of information.

reviews.summary: This profile is meticulously researched and well-structured, with robust sourcing from credible outlets like official government websites, Ballotpedia, and reputable news sources such as AP News and Emerson College Polling. It offers balanced, detailed coverage of candidates' backgrounds, career histories, and issue stances, while thoughtfully noting gaps or uncertainties, such as 'no public position found' for certain issues, demonstrating a thorough and fair approach to handling incomplete information.
